jokes.text: Hear about Miss Hoover? She drank some drain cleaner. / I heard she fell down a well.
jokes.setup: Students speculating about their absent teacher
jokes.punchline: Each rumor is more ridiculous than the last
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of absurd rumors, economical delivery, but generic kid characters

jokes.text: She's been dumped again.
jokes.setup: Wild rumors about teacher's absence
jokes.punchline: Mundane, relatable reality delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of drama with mundane truth, very economical

jokes.text: I won't be staying long. I just learned I have Lyme disease.
jokes.setup: Teacher returns immediately after being rumored dead/injured
jokes.punchline: Announces she's leaving again for hypochondriac reason
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-specific humor, sets up Miss Hoover as hypochondriac

jokes.text: When a tick begins to suck your blood... spirochetes infest your blood and spinal fluid and then the brain.
jokes.setup: Child asked to explain a disease
jokes.punchline: Lisa delivers graphic medical description with clinical detachment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa character moment - precocious knowledge delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Bart Simpson, I know it's you!
jokes.setup: Skinner substitute teaching, something disruptive happening
jokes.punchline: Immediately identifies Bart as the troublemaker without looking
jokes.explanation: Efficient character establishment - Skinner knows his troublemakers, Bart's reputation precedes him

jokes.text: And here comes Snowball II. We kept this one. The mother ate the gray one.
jokes.setup: Bart showing what appears to be home movies
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of pet infanticide/cannibalism
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor delivered with perfect casual tone, very quotable and memorable

jokes.text: Look, this is cool. When I hit reverse, I can make them go back in.
jokes.setup: Bart showing disturbing animal footage
jokes.punchline: Treats graphic birth/death as entertainment, reversible
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to establish Bart's morbid fascination, visual element implied

jokes.text: No, you're not seeing things. This, my little friends, is a schwa.
jokes.setup: New substitute teacher making dramatic entrance
jokes.punchline: Theatrical presentation of mundane grammar concept
jokes.explanation: Great character introduction - makes grammar exciting, establishes Bergstrom's teaching style

jokes.text: Are you the substitute? / I am. / Are you insane? / It's how I get their attention.
jokes.setup: Student questions teacher's dramatic methods
jokes.punchline: Teacher acknowledges the apparent insanity as intentional pedagogy
jokes.explanation: Clean setup/punchline structure, good character insight about unconventional teaching

jokes.text: Howdy. I'm a Texas cowboy from 1830. / Can we play kickball? / Kickball? There ain't no kickball in 1830.
jokes.setup: Teacher in cowboy costume offering historical roleplay
jokes.punchline: Student immediately asks about modern activity, teacher stays in character
jokes.explanation: Good commitment to character bit, shows kids' priorities vs. education

jokes.text: Kickball? There ain't no kickball in 1830. Any other questions? / Shoot! It's awfully quiet.
jokes.setup: Teacher staying in character to answer kickball question
jokes.punchline: Awkward silence after shutting down fun activity
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bergstrom's commitment and the resulting awkwardness

jokes.text: There are three things wrong with my costume. Name them and you get my hat.
jokes.setup: Teacher offering reward for spotting historical inaccuracies
jokes.punchline: Turns costume into interactive learning exercise
jokes.explanation: Clever teaching method, shows Bergstrom's engaging style

jokes.text: Your belt says 'State of Texas,' but it wasn't a state until 1845. / The revolver wasn't around till 1835.
jokes.setup: Lisa analyzing historical costume for inaccuracies
jokes.punchline: Demonstrates encyclopedic knowledge of minutiae
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa moment - shows her intelligence and attention to detail

jokes.text: You seem to be Jewish. / Jewish. / Cowboys aren't Jewish.
jokes.setup: Lisa identifying third historical inaccuracy
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact observation about religious demographics in Old West
jokes.explanation: Shows Lisa's analytical mind, introduces Bergstrom's background naturally

jokes.text: For the record, there were some Jewish cowboys. Big guys who spent money freely.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Jewish cowboys being historically inaccurate
jokes.punchline: Dry correction with stereotypical characterization
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery, mild stereotype but in service of historical accuracy

jokes.text: You can make fun of my name. Two suggestions are Mr. Nerd-strom and Mr. Booger-strom.
jokes.setup: Teacher introducing himself to class
jokes.punchline: Preemptively provides childish name variations
jokes.explanation: Brilliant teaching psychology - disarms kids by acknowledging the obvious, shows his understanding of children

jokes.text: Asimov, Bester, Clarke-- / What about Bradbury? / I'm aware of his work.
jokes.setup: Martin listing sci-fi authors alphabetically
jokes.punchline: Dismissive response to Bradbury suggestion
jokes.explanation: Good Martin character moment - pedantic, hierarchical view of literature

jokes.text: Actually, the range was far from home. It was a desolate place of danger and disease.
jokes.setup: Correcting romanticized cowboy song lyrics
jokes.punchline: Realistic description of harsh frontier life
jokes.explanation: Mild humor from historical reality vs. mythology, fits Bergstrom's teaching style

jokes.text: Unlike the efficient Indians, cowboys used only the tongue of the antelope.
jokes.setup: Comparing Native American vs. cowboy resource use
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of wasteful/gross eating habits
jokes.explanation: Educational humor with mild gross-out factor, historical accuracy

jokes.text: What's this? Did you do it? / No! I would never do anything like that. / Can I have it? / Yes, but I didn't do it.
jokes.setup: Teacher finding Lisa's creative work, Lisa denying responsibility
jokes.punchline: Bergstrom wants to keep it despite Lisa's protestations
jokes.explanation: Shows both characters - Lisa's modesty, Bergstrom's appreciation for creativity

jokes.text: Class, 'The Singing Dork.' / Lisa!
jokes.setup: Teacher about to share Lisa's private creative work
jokes.punchline: Lisa's mortified reaction to public exposure
jokes.explanation: Relatable school embarrassment, good character moment for both

jokes.text: I had a speech, but my dog ate it.
jokes.setup: Bart asked to give campaign speech
jokes.punchline: Classic excuse delivered as campaign platform
jokes.explanation: Clever use of tired excuse as political strategy, very economical

jokes.text: Would you do it? / But where's the principal's office?
jokes.setup: Teacher asking Bart to deliver note to principal
jokes.punchline: Bart pretends not to know where principal's office is
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart moment - troublemaker pretending innocence, everyone knows he's been there many times

jokes.text: What did I say about encouraging him? When Bart wins approval for being a fool--
jokes.setup: Students cheering for Bart's antics
jokes.punchline: Teacher recognizing the psychological dynamic at play
jokes.explanation: Shows Bergstrom's psychological insight, understands classroom dynamics

jokes.text: Come on, everybody has a talent. / I wanna see yours. / I don't have one.
jokes.setup: Teacher encouraging students to show talents
jokes.punchline: Brilliant Lisa claims to have no talent
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Lisa's modesty and insecurity despite her obvious abilities

jokes.text: Chuck? / Disgusting. I love it!
jokes.setup: Student presumably showing gross talent
jokes.punchline: Teacher's enthusiastic approval of disgusting display
jokes.explanation: Shows Bergstrom's unconventional teaching style, appreciates all forms of expression

jokes.text: While I'm technically married, it hasn't been much of a marriage since Mr. Krabappel moved out.
jokes.setup: Mrs. Krabappel hitting on substitute teacher
jokes.punchline: TMI about her failed marriage
jokes.explanation: Establishes Mrs. Krabappel's desperation and oversharing tendencies

jokes.text: Mrs. Krabappel, you're trying to seduce me. / Well? / I'm sorry. You're very nice, but it's the children I love.
jokes.setup: Bergstrom calling out seduction attempt
jokes.punchline: Gentle rejection emphasizing dedication to teaching
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - shows Bergstrom's professionalism and dedication

jokes.text: When he smiles, you see these teeth. But when he laughs, you see these. The eyeteeth.
jokes.setup: Lisa describing her crush on Mr. Bergstrom
jokes.punchline: Hyper-specific observation about different teeth visibility
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa moment - analytical mind applied to childhood crush, very specific and relatable

jokes.text: Yesterday, he read us Charlotte's Web and cried at the end. / A book made him cry!
jokes.setup: Lisa describing Bergstrom's emotional response to literature
jokes.punchline: Marge's amazed reaction to male emotional expression
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between sensitive teacher and Marge's experience with Homer's emotional range

jokes.text: Your homework is always so neat. Does your father help you? / Homework's not his specialty.
jokes.setup: Teacher asking about parental homework help
jokes.punchline: Lisa's diplomatic way of saying Homer is useless
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa diplomacy - acknowledges Homer's limitations without being mean

jokes.text: Unless the next word was 'burp,' don't bother.
jokes.setup: Bergstrom trying to defend Homer's potential
jokes.punchline: Lisa's brutally honest assessment of Homer's intellectual contributions
jokes.explanation: Excellent Lisa line - perfectly captures Homer's intellectual level with devastating economy

jokes.text: That's not enough! We demand more asbestos! More asbestos!
jokes.setup: Learning about dangerous asbestos levels in school
jokes.punchline: Students demand MORE of the dangerous substance
jokes.explanation: Dark absurdist humor - children misunderstanding safety information, very Simpsons-esque

jokes.text: Just a popularity contest? What's more important than popularity?
jokes.setup: Bart dismissing election as mere popularity contest
jokes.punchline: Homer's genuine confusion about what could be more important
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - shows his value system and social priorities

jokes.text: I knew you had personality! They said it was hyperactivity, but I knew better!
jokes.setup: Homer praising Bart's election chances
jokes.punchline: Reframes behavioral problems as positive traits
jokes.explanation: Good Homer parenting moment - positive spin on concerning behavior

jokes.text: He says, there are no easy answers! I say, he's not looking hard enough!
jokes.setup: Bart giving campaign speech
jokes.punchline: Simplistic solution to complex problems
jokes.explanation: Excellent political satire - captures populist campaign rhetoric perfectly

jokes.text: You never lose appealing to the lowest common denominator.
jokes.setup: Bart's simplistic campaign succeeding
jokes.punchline: Bergstrom's cynical but accurate political observation
jokes.explanation: Good political commentary, shows Bergstrom's wisdom about human nature

jokes.text: I believe everything you say, with your words and Semitic good looks.
jokes.setup: Lisa expressing trust in Bergstrom
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate comment about his Jewish appearance
jokes.explanation: Awkward child crush moment - Lisa says inappropriate thing without understanding

jokes.text: Kevin's biting me. Here's a drawing of a spirochete. Love, Ralph.
jokes.setup: Ralph writing get-well card to Miss Hoover
jokes.punchline: Mix of childish complaint and sophisticated medical illustration
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ralph moment - combines childish concerns with inexplicably advanced knowledge

jokes.text: So it's up to me, is it? / Yes. / And you think that people are gonna pay you $4.50 when they don't have to?
jokes.setup: Museum has 'suggested donation' policy
jokes.punchline: Homer's incredulous reaction to honor system
jokes.explanation: Good Homer moment - shows his cheap nature and skepticism about human nature

jokes.text: Anything you say! Good luck, lady! You'll need it!
jokes.setup: Museum clerk explaining donation policy
jokes.punchline: Homer's sarcastic farewell predicting business failure
jokes.explanation: Shows Homer's pessimism about honor systems, good character consistency

jokes.text: His teeth were jagged, but he could swallow you whole.
jokes.setup: Describing prehistoric predator
jokes.punchline: Vivid, slightly disturbing description for children
jokes.explanation: Bergstrom making learning vivid and memorable, slightly dark humor

jokes.text: First, they pulled the brain out and stuffed the skull with onions.
jokes.setup: Explaining Egyptian mummification process
jokes.punchline: Graphic, absurd historical detail
jokes.explanation: Educational gross-out humor, accurate historical detail made funny

jokes.text: She sees everybody else's dad with a good education and says, 'What did I do to deserve this?'
jokes.setup: Homer discussing Lisa's need for role model
jokes.punchline: Homer imagining Lisa's thoughts about having him as father
jokes.explanation: Rare moment of Homer self-awareness, touching and funny simultaneously

jokes.text: She sees everybody else's dad... with a good education and says, 'What did I do to deserve this?'
jokes.setup: Homer talking about Lisa's feelings
jokes.punchline: His surprisingly accurate self-assessment
jokes.explanation: Rare moment of Homer showing genuine insight into his limitations as a father

jokes.text: You are smooth. I'll give you that.
jokes.setup: Bergstrom explaining Lisa earned her grade
jokes.punchline: Homer thinks Bergstrom is conning him somehow
jokes.explanation: Homer's suspicious nature misinterpreting honest teaching praise

jokes.text: Can I wear jewelry? / Sure. / Dye my shoes? Paint my nails? Can we have wine? Can I have wine? Does Bart have to be there?
jokes.setup: Lisa excited about dinner with Bergstrom
jokes.punchline: Increasingly inappropriate requests for eight-year-old
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Lisa treating dinner like adult date, good structure

jokes.text: Mr. Bergstrom, do you like pork chops? Oh, no, of course not.
jokes.setup: Marge trying to invite Bergstrom to dinner
jokes.punchline: Realizes dietary restrictions mid-sentence
jokes.explanation: Relatable social awkwardness, religious/cultural sensitivity fail

jokes.text: My Lyme disease turned out to be psychosomatic. / Does that mean you're crazy? / It means she was faking. / It was a little of both.
jokes.setup: Miss Hoover explains her illness was psychosomatic
jokes.punchline: Admits to being both crazy and faking
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to earlier setup, Miss Hoover's self-aware admission

jokes.text: What did he teach you? / That life is worth living.
jokes.setup: Miss Hoover asking about substitute's curriculum
jokes.punchline: Lisa's profound response about life lessons vs. academics
jokes.explanation: Beautiful contrast between bureaucratic concern and genuine education impact

jokes.text: Victory party under the slide!
jokes.setup: Bart giving final campaign statement
jokes.punchline: Promises party in playground equipment
jokes.explanation: Good kid-level campaign promise, appropriately small-scale

jokes.text: The train. How like him. Traditional, yet environmentally sound.
jokes.setup: Learning Bergstrom is taking the train
jokes.punchline: Lisa's romanticizing of mundane transportation choice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa - intellectualizing and romanticizing simple choices

jokes.text: And it's been the backbone of our country since Stanford drove in that golden spike.
jokes.setup: Continuing train discussion
jokes.punchline: Historical reference with wrong name (should be Stanford, but implies Leland Stanford)
jokes.explanation: Mild historical reference humor, shows Skinner's tendency to lecture

jokes.text: I see he touched you too.
jokes.setup: Skinner observing Lisa's reaction to Bergstrom leaving
jokes.punchline: Unintentionally suggestive phrasing about teacher's influence
jokes.explanation: Unintentional double entendre creates awkward moment

jokes.text: Thanks for your vote. / Voting's for geeks! / You got that right.
jokes.setup: Martin thanking students for votes
jokes.punchline: Students agree voting is for geeks while having just voted
jokes.explanation: Good irony about anti-intellectual attitude immediately after participating

jokes.text: I demand a re-count. / Two for Martin. Want another re-count?
jokes.setup: Bart losing election badly demands recount
jokes.punchline: Second count confirms pathetic results
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Bart's humiliation, bureaucratic efficiency humor

jokes.text: It's the life of the substitute teacher. He's a fraud. Today he wears gym shorts, tomorrow he speaks French or runs a band saw or God knows what.
jokes.setup: Explaining why he's leaving
jokes.punchline: Self-deprecating description of substitute teacher's jack-of-all-trades existence
jokes.explanation: Good insight into substitute teaching reality, escalating absurdity of subjects

jokes.text: When you're middle-class, anybody who cares will leave to help someone needier.
jokes.setup: Lisa saying she needs him too
jokes.punchline: Cynical but accurate observation about middle-class abandonment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant social commentary disguised as simple dialogue, very quotable and insightful

jokes.text: If you don't mind, I'll run alongside the train as it speeds you from my life.
jokes.setup: Lisa's farewell to Bergstrom
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic, literary farewell inappropriate for eight-year-old
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa - inappropriately sophisticated language for her age, romanticizing the moment

jokes.text: If you don't mind, I'll run alongside the train... as it speeds you from my life.
jokes.setup: Lisa saying goodbye to Bergstrom
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic and cinematic farewell
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa moment showing her dramatic flair and romantic notions

jokes.text: Just because I don't care doesn't mean I don't understand.
jokes.setup: Lisa saying Homer wouldn't understand
jokes.punchline: Homer's brutally honest self-assessment
jokes.explanation: Exceptional Homer line - perfectly captures his character, incredibly quotable, great structure

jokes.text: But you, sir, are a baboon! / Me? / Yes, you! Baboon! Baboon! Baboon!
jokes.setup: Lisa's emotional breakdown
jokes.punchline: Repeated accusation with increasing intensity
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and repetition for comic effect, shows Lisa's emotional state

jokes.text: The stupidest and ugliest ape of all!
jokes.setup: Homer hurt by being called baboon
jokes.punchline: Homer knows exactly what a baboon is taxonomically
jokes.explanation: Shows Homer's unexpected knowledge while emphasizing the insult's impact

jokes.text: Everyone special to me is under this roof.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining he's never lost anyone special
jokes.punchline: Simple but profound statement about family
jokes.explanation: Beautiful Homer moment - shows his limited world but deep family love

jokes.text: Maybe I can't explain all this, but I can fix your dollhouse. At least I'm good at monkey work.
jokes.setup: Homer struggling with emotional conversation
jokes.punchline: Reduces himself to manual labor, references baboon insult
jokes.explanation: Good callback to baboon insult, shows Homer's self-awareness and practical love

jokes.text: You know, monkey? You know what I mean? I can hold these nails in place with my tail.
jokes.setup: Homer continuing monkey references while working
jokes.punchline: Absurd claim about using imaginary tail as tool
jokes.explanation: Continues monkey theme, physical comedy implied, good father-daughter bonding

jokes.text: If just me, Milhouse and Lewis had voted. / Would you have gotten any money for being class president? / No. / Would you have more work? / Yeah.
jokes.setup: Bart lamenting his election loss
jokes.punchline: Homer's practical analysis of the 'benefits' of losing
jokes.explanation: Good Homer wisdom - practical analysis helps Bart see he's better off losing

jokes.text: Hey, thanks, monkey-man.
jokes.setup: Bart thanking Homer for the advice
jokes.punchline: Affectionate reference to earlier monkey jokes
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that shows the monkey insult has become term of endearment

jokes.text: Holy moly, talk about parenting.
jokes.setup: Homer successfully helping both children
jokes.punchline: Self-congratulatory observation about his unexpected success
jokes.explanation: Good Homer moment - rare self-awareness about actually being a good parent

jokes.text: Let's just go to bed. I'm on the biggest roll of my life.
jokes.setup: Marge about to ask about his success
jokes.punchline: Homer recognizing his unprecedented parenting streak
jokes.explanation: Great episode ending - Homer's awareness that this level of parenting success is unusual for him
